title: Act on the Acquisition and Improvement of Professional Competence for Driving Motor Vehicles and on Amendments to Certain Acts

parts.ODBORNÁ ZPŮSOBILOST ŘIDIČŮ MOTOROVÝCH VO: PROFESSIONAL COMPETENCE OF MOTOR VEHICLE DRIVERS
parts.ZKOUŠKY ODBORNÉ ZPŮSOBILOSTI ŽADATELŮ O: EXAMINATIONS OF PROFESSIONAL COMPETENCE OF APPLICANTS FOR DRIVING LICENCES

chapters.OPRÁVNĚNÍ K PROVÁDĚNÍ VÝUKY A VÝCVIKU: AUTHORIZATION TO CONDUCT INSTRUCTION AND TRAINING
chapters.PODMÍNKY PŘIJETÍ K VÝUCE A VÝCVIKU K ZÍS: CONDITIONS FOR ADMISSION TO INSTRUCTION AND TRAINING FOR OBTAINING A DRIVING LICENCE AND TYPES OF INSTRUCTION AND TRAINING
chapters.ZDOKONALOVÁNÍ ODBORNÉ ZPŮSOBILOSTI ŘIDIČ: IMPROVEMENT OF DRIVERS' PROFESSIONAL COMPETENCE FOR THE PURPOSES OF DRIVERS' PROFESSIONAL QUALIFICATION
IMPROVEMENT OF DRIVERS' PROFESSIONAL COMPETENCE BY PARTICIPATING IN TRAINING FOR NOVICE DRIVERS
IMPROVEMENT OF PROFESSIONAL COMPETENCE OF DRIVERS WHO HAVE POINTS RECORDED IN THE DRIVER REGISTER FOR CONDUCT INCLUDED IN THE POINT ASSESSMENT SYSTEM
chapters.ZPŮSOB VÝUKY A VÝCVIKU ŽADATELŮ O ŘIDIČS: METHOD OF INSTRUCTION AND TRAINING FOR APPLICANTS FOR DRIVING LICENCES AND THEIR COMPLETION

p1.heading: Subject matter
p1.text:
This Act incorporates relevant European Union regulations^1) and regulates

- a) conditions for the operation of driving schools,

- b) the method of conducting instruction and training for applicants for obtaining professional competence to drive a motor vehicle (hereinafter referred to as „obtaining a driving licence“) and the rights and obligations of applicants for obtaining a driving licence,

- c) the rights and obligations of driving school operators and instructors,

- d) conditions for granting and withdrawing certificates for instruction and training instructors (hereinafter referred to as „professional certificates“) and examiner's permits,

- e) the method of conducting examinations for obtaining a driving licence,

- f) the method of conducting driver instruction and training as part of improving drivers' professional competence,

- g) requirements for a natural or legal person who conducts instruction and training as part of improving drivers' professional competence, and their rights and obligations,

- h) the issuance of a driver's professional qualification card,

- i) re-examination of professional competence to drive motor vehicles in the point assessment system,

- j) the scope of authority of administrative bodies and state supervision.

p2.text:
(1) The operation of a driving school means providing instruction and training for obtaining a driving licence. A driving school may be operated by a legal or natural person on the basis of a trade licence issued under a special act^1a) and registration for the operation of a driving school. Registration for the operation of a driving school is issued by the municipal office of a municipality with extended powers. The competent authority is the municipal office of a municipality with extended powers, according to the location of the applicant's establishment. The operator of a driving school may also be an organizational unit of the state that ensures the defense and security of the state (hereinafter referred to as „organizational unit of the state“). An organizational unit of the state may conduct instruction and training for obtaining a driving licence and improving professional competence only for its employees and persons in service relationships or employees and persons in service relationships of other organizational units of the state ensuring the defense and security of the state, unless another legal regulation stipulates otherwise.

(2) The municipal office of a municipality with extended powers shall issue registration for the operation of a driving school on the basis of a written application, if the applicant proves that

- a) they are the holder of the relevant trade licence, or in the case where the applicant is an organizational unit of the state, they are the holder of an establishing deed from which it follows that they conduct instruction and training for obtaining a licence to drive vehicles,

- b) they have met the technical conditions for the operation of a driving school according to this Act.

(3) Upon fulfillment of these conditions, the applicant is entitled to the issuance of registration for the operation of a driving school.

(4) The municipal office of a municipality with extended powers shall decide on the registration for the operation of a driving school within 60 days.

p3.text:
(1) An application for registration to operate a driving school must contain

- a) the business name, registered office and legal form of the legal entity and its identification number (hereinafter referred to as „identification number“), if the applicant is a legal entity, or the first name, or first names (hereinafter referred to as „first name“), surname and permanent residence, identification number, business name and date of birth of the natural person, if the applicant is a natural person,

- b) the scope of instruction and training provided,

- c) the first name, surname, permanent residence and date of birth of the natural persons who will provide instruction and training,

- d) the first name, surname, permanent residence and date of birth of the natural persons who will provide medical preparation,

- e) the method of securing training vehicles,

- f) a description and location of the driving range or other training area with the exclusion of other vehicle traffic (hereinafter referred to as „training area“) or driving simulator, and the address and designation of the instruction and teaching premises.

(2) An application for registration to operate a driving school must be accompanied by

- a) a document proving the applicant's legal relationship to the driving range or training area or driving simulator, to the instruction and teaching premises and to the training vehicles,

- b) valid professional certificates of persons who will conduct instruction and training for obtaining a driving licence,

- c) a trade licence or, in the case where the applicant is an organizational unit of the state, an establishing deed.

(3) The documents listed in paragraph 2 shall be submitted by the applicant in certified copies or originals for inspection.

p4.text:
(1) In the decision on the issuance of a driving school operation registration, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall state:

- a) the business name, including the legal form, registered office, and identification number, if it is a legal entity, or the name and surname of the driving school operator, date of birth, identification number, trade name, and permanent residence; if a responsible representative is appointed, also information concerning that person,

- b) the designation of the teaching and training premises,

- c) the scope of provided instruction and training,

- d) the deadline for commencing driving school operations,

- e) the technical conditions for driving school operations.

(2) If the application has been rejected, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall state in the decision the reasons for which the driving school operation registration was not issued.

(3) The authorization to operate a driving school also arises from the fruitless expiry of the deadline and in the manner specified in § 28 to 30 of the Act on the Free Movement of Services.

p5.text:
(1) The driving school operator is obliged to notify the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers of all changes concerning the data and documents that are stipulated as requirements for the application for the issuance of a driving school operation registration pursuant to § 3, and to submit documents thereof within 15 days from the occurrence of the changes.

(2) The municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall decide on the change of data specified in the decision based on the notified changes pursuant to paragraph 1. The municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall issue a new registration certificate if the changes concern data specified in § 4 para. 1 letter a) to c).

p6.heading: Withdrawal of driving school operation registration
p6.text:
(1) The municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall decide on the withdrawal of a driving school operation registration if the driving school operator

- a) repeatedly or grossly violated obligations stipulated by this Act or on its basis,

- b) fails to comply with the conditions stipulated in the driving school operation registration,

- c) ceased to meet the conditions stipulated in § 2 para. 2,

- d) requested the withdrawal of the driving school operation registration.

(2) A driving school operator whose driving school operation registration has been withdrawn pursuant to paragraph 1 letter a) or b) may reapply to the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers for registration no earlier than 3 years after the decision on the withdrawal of the driving school operation registration.

p7.heading: Technical conditions for driving school operation
p7.text:
A driving school operation registration ceases to be valid

- a) on the day of the dissolution of the legal entity that is the holder of the driving school operation registration,

- b) on the day of the termination of the trade license in the event of the death of a natural person who is the holder of the driving school operation registration,

- c) on the day on which the decision of the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers on the withdrawal of the driving school operation registration became final.

p8.text:
(1) The technical conditions for driving school operation mean

- a) a driving range or practice area or a driving simulator,

- b) premises for instruction and training,

- c) teaching aids,

- d) a training vehicle.

(2) The technical conditions for driving ranges, practice areas, and teaching aids for practical maintenance instruction are listed in Annex No. 1.

p8a.text:
(1) Only non-residential premises meeting the conditions for an establishment according to a special legal regulation^1a) may be used as teaching and training premises.

(2) The driving school operator must have the right to use the teaching and training premises for the entire duration of the driving school operation and must have these premises available at all times.

p9.text:
(1) In a driving school, for instruction and training to obtain a driving license, a training vehicle may be operated,

- a) which, by its design and technical condition, meets the requirements stipulated by a special legal regulation,^2)

- b) which is equipped with a dual device of approved design according to a special legal regulation^2) enabling the control of the clutch and service brake of the vehicle, or the accelerator; for a vehicle with an automatic transmission, a dual device for controlling the service brake of the vehicle is sufficient; if it is a tractor, an additional seat for the training instructor must also be placed in its cabin, the placement and design of which must be approved according to a special legal regulation,^2) if it is a motorcycle, in addition to a dual device enabling the control of the clutch and service brake, it must be equipped with dual steering of approved design according to a special legal regulation,^2) or an electronic device enabling communication between the instructor and the student and control of the motorcycle's ignition,

- c) which is equipped with an additional interior mirror, if prescribed for the given type of vehicle,

- d) which has been approved by the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers for the purposes of instruction and training.

(2) The types of training vehicles for instruction, training, and examination of professional competence to drive motor vehicles for the relevant group of motor vehicles according to a special legal regulation^12) (hereinafter referred to as “vehicle group”) and the technical requirements for training vehicles are listed in Annex No. 2 to this Act.

(3) In a driving school, a vehicle structurally adapted for a physically disabled person, which is not a training vehicle, may be used as a training vehicle.

(4) If this Act so stipulates, a motorcycle that does not meet the conditions according to paragraph 1 letter b) may be used as a training vehicle.

p10.text:
(1) A training vehicle shall be approved for use in driver training by the authority competent for the registration of road motor vehicles pursuant to a special act.^2) The authority shall approve the training vehicle based on a written application from the vehicle operator.

(2) The application must contain

- a) the business name, registered office, and legal form of the legal entity and its identification number, if the applicant is a legal entity, or the first name, surname, permanent residence, and date of birth of the natural person, if the applicant is a natural person,

- b) the make of the vehicle,

- c) the year of manufacture of the vehicle,

- d) the registration plate of the vehicle.

(3) The application must be accompanied by

- a) written consent from the vehicle owner, if the owner is not the applicant,

- b) documents proving a change in the recorded data.

(4) After approving the vehicle for training, the authority shall make an official record in the register of road vehicles with the text „Approved as a training vehicle“, issue a new certificate of registration of a road motor vehicle and trailer, in which it shall record the change in vehicle use in the „Other records“ section, and mark the date of the next regular technical inspection and emission measurement on the vehicle's registration plate with a sticker.^2)

(5) The vehicle operator is obliged to notify the competent authority in writing of a change in vehicle use before decommissioning a training vehicle from training. The notification must be accompanied by written consent from the vehicle owner, if the owner is not the applicant.

(6) When decommissioning a vehicle from training, the authority shall mark the change in vehicle use in the manner specified in paragraph 4.

p11.text:
(1) A training vehicle, with the exception of a motorcycle, and the motorcycle crew must be properly marked during driver training and during the final examination. The marking of the training vehicle must be placed on the vehicle so that it is visible to other road users from all sides, and for the motorcycle crew, from the front and rear, at a distance of at least 50 meters.

(2) The method of marking a training vehicle according to the types of training vehicles, the method of marking a motorcycle crew, and the material from which the marking must be made shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p12.text: The driver of a training vehicle, when using the training vehicle for their own needs, must remove or cover the training vehicle's marking with an opaque cover.

p13.heading: Conditions for admission to instruction and training
p13.text:
(1) Instruction and training for obtaining a driving licence may be provided exclusively by a driving school operator. A driving school operator shall admit to instruction and training for obtaining a driving licence a person who

- a) submits a written application; if the applicant is under 15 years of age, the application must be accompanied by the written consent of their legal guardian, whose signature has been verified by the competent authority,

- b) by the date of completion of instruction and training, reaches the age prescribed for the granting of a driving licence for the relevant group according to a special act,^2a) provided that the period from the commencement of instruction and training to their completion must not exceed 18 months,

- c) is not incapacitated for legal acts,

- d) is medically fit to drive a motor vehicle according to a special act,^2a)

- e) fulfils other conditions, if required by a special act^2a) for obtaining a driving licence,

- f) is not serving an administrative penalty or a penalty of prohibition of activity consisting of a prohibition on driving motor vehicles.

(2) Students of a school for which a registration to operate a driving school has been issued, for whom obtaining a driving licence is a qualification prerequisite for the performance of the profession for which the students are preparing at the school, or for whom driving motor vehicles is an optional (non-optional) subject, may be enrolled in instruction and training no earlier than 2 years before reaching the prescribed age for the granting of a driving licence for the relevant vehicle group.^2a) They may then take the professional competence examination after completing instruction and training, even before reaching the prescribed age, with the proviso that the driving licence will be issued to them upon reaching it. The said schools may then provide instruction and training as combined in the sense of § 16 para. 1, whereby when combining vehicle groups listed in § 15 para. 1, group C or C1 may also be added.

(3) An application for admission to instruction and training shall contain

- a) the first name, or names, and surname of the applicant,

- b) the address of the applicant's residence,

- c) the applicant's date of birth,

- d) the vehicle group for which the applicant already holds a driving licence,

- e) the vehicle group for which the applicant applies for admission to instruction and training.

(4) The applicant shall attach to the application for admission to instruction and training a document of their medical fitness to drive motor vehicles, which must not be older than 3 months on the date of submission of the application.

(5) The applicant shall attest to the facts stated in paragraph 1 letters e) and f) by an affidavit.

(6) The form of the application for admission to instruction and training shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p14.heading: Types of instruction and training
p14.text:
(1) A driving school operator is authorized to provide the following types of instruction and training within the scope stipulated in the registration for operating a driving school:

- a) basic instruction and training,

- b) combined instruction and training,

- c) extended instruction and training,

- d) instruction and training according to an individual study plan,

- e) supplementary instruction and training.

(2) The driving school operator shall ensure that the applicant for a driving licence acquires theoretical knowledge and practical skills through instruction and training so that they are capable of

- a) driving a vehicle in accordance with road traffic regulations,

- b) controlling a vehicle so as not to create dangerous situations and reacting appropriately to their occurrence,

- c) recognizing operational hazards and their severity and being able to react to these situations promptly and correctly,

- d) recognizing technical defects in vehicles that pose a threat to safety,

- e) reacting to factors influencing driving behaviour and maintaining the abilities necessary for safe driving,

- f) providing effective first aid to injured persons in a traffic accident.

p15.heading: Basic instruction and training
p15.text:
(1) Basic instruction and training is the preparation of an applicant for obtaining a driving licence for group AM, A1, A2, A, B1, B or T.

(2) The minimum number of hours of basic instruction and training is specified in Annex No. 3.

p16.heading: Combined instruction and training
p16.text:
(1) "Combined instruction and training" means the preparation of an applicant for obtaining a driving licence for a combination of 2 or more vehicle categories. Combined instruction and training may be provided if the applicant meets the conditions stipulated by a special act for individual vehicle categories within the given combination of combined instruction and training.

(2) An applicant for a driving licence must acquire theoretical and practical knowledge through combined instruction and training as if they had completed instruction and training for each vehicle category within the given combination of combined instruction and training separately.

(3) Combined instruction and training, with the exception of vehicle driving training, shall be carried out to the extent stipulated by the curriculum for the highest number of teaching hours for the vehicle category in the given combination of combined instruction and training. This scope of teaching hours shall be proportionally extended by the necessary number of teaching hours required for the instruction and training of topics specific to each additional vehicle category in the given combination of combined instruction and training.

(4) Vehicle driving training shall be carried out to the extent stipulated for each vehicle category in the given combination of combined instruction and training.

p17.heading: Extended instruction and training
p17.text:
(1) "Extended instruction and training" means the preparation of an applicant for extending an already obtained driving licence to include another vehicle category. Extended instruction and training cannot be provided to an applicant who has obtained a driving licence exclusively for category AM or category T and applies for obtaining a driving licence for another vehicle category.

(2) Within the scope of extended instruction and training, a combination of extended and combined instruction and training may be carried out pursuant to § 16.

(3) The minimum number of hours of extended instruction and training is listed in Annex No. 3.

p18.heading: Instruction and training according to an individual study plan
p18.text:
(1) "Instruction and training according to an individual study plan" means the preparation of an applicant for obtaining a driving licence for all vehicle categories with the exception of categories D, D+E, D1 and D1+E.

(2) The applicant for a driving licence shall acquire theoretical preparation through self-study and compulsory consultations to the extent stipulated by the curriculum.

(3) When instructing according to an individual study plan, at least one teaching hour of consultations must be provided for every four hours of instruction stipulated by the curriculum for the given vehicle category. A group may have a maximum of 5 students.

(4) The scope of training must not be less than the number of teaching hours stipulated by the curriculum for the given vehicle category.

p19.heading: Supplementary instruction and training
p19.text:
Supplementary instruction and training may only be provided to an applicant for a driving licence who

- a) has completed instruction and training for obtaining a driving licence, but has not yet been granted a driving licence pursuant to a special act,^2a) for a maximum period specified in the provision of § 39 para. 6 of this Act,

- b) applies for

- 1. extending a driving licence from category A1 to category A2,

- 2. extending a driving licence from category A2 to category A, or

- 3. extending the scope of a driving licence for category B to include vehicle combinations consisting of a category B vehicle and a trailer with a maximum authorised mass exceeding 750 kg, whose maximum authorised mass exceeds 3,500 kg but does not exceed 4,250 kg, or

- c) applies for extending a driving licence with a restriction to vehicles equipped with an automatic transmission to a driving licence without this restriction.

p20.heading: Curriculum for instruction and training of applicants for driving licences
p20.text:
(1) The purpose of instruction and training for applicants for driving licences is to acquire the necessary theoretical and practical knowledge, skills, and habits for driving motor vehicles in road traffic.

(2) Instruction includes

- a) instruction on vehicle traffic regulations,

- b) instruction on vehicle operation and maintenance,

- c) instruction on driving theory and principles of safe driving,

- d) instruction on medical preparation.

(3) Training includes

- a) vehicle driving training,

- b) practical vehicle maintenance training,

- c) practical medical preparation training.

(4) The scope of instruction and training is divided according to the type of instruction and training and according to the individual vehicle categories for which the instruction and training are provided.

(5) The content and scope of individual types of instruction and training for obtaining driving licences shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p21.text:
(1) Individual subjects, with the exception of the subject of medical preparation, may be taught by persons who hold a professional certificate. The Regional Authority decides on the issuance of a professional certificate. In the professional certificate, the Regional Authority shall state the subjects that the teacher is authorized to teach. The Regional Authority shall keep records of issued professional certificates to the extent of the data stated therein. The model of the professional certificate shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) A person who meets the following conditions may be a holder of a professional certificate:

- a) has completed secondary education with a matriculation examination, or, in the case of a driving instructor or practical vehicle maintenance instructor, has completed secondary education,

- b) has reached the age of 24,

- c) has successfully undergone a traffic-psychological examination; traffic-psychological examinations are carried out by natural persons who provide psychological counseling services according to a special legal regulation^2b) or legal entities for whom traffic-psychological counseling is the subject of their activity (hereinafter referred to as “traffic psychologist”),

- d) has completed basic training and demonstrated knowledge of road traffic regulations, conditions for vehicle operation on roads, obtaining and improving professional competence for driving motor vehicles, and knowledge of vehicle control and maintenance, vehicle driving, and principles of safe vehicle driving, including a vehicle structurally adapted for a physically disabled person; in the case of teaching road traffic regulations and the theory of driving and principles of safe driving, this person must hold a driving license for the vehicle group for which they provide instruction,

- e) has not been sentenced by a court or administrative authority to a ban on driving motor vehicles and has not been subject to such a sentence in the last three years.

(3) Training may be provided by a person who:

- a) meets the conditions specified in paragraph 2 letters a) to e), and

- b) has held a driving license for at least 3 years for the vehicle group for which they provide training.

p22.text:
Teaching medical preparation and providing practical first aid training may be carried out by:

- a) doctors,

- b) higher healthcare professionals in the fields of activity of a certified general nurse, certified pediatric nurse, and certified midwife,

- c) secondary healthcare professionals in the fields of activity of a paramedic, general nurse, pediatric nurse, midwife, health nurse, women's nurse, and intensive care nurse,

- d) lower healthcare professionals in the field of activity of an orderly, nursing assistant, ambulance driver, and emergency medical vehicle driver,

- e) teachers of vocational subjects in nursing at secondary and higher medical schools,

- f) graduates of retraining courses accredited by the Ministry of Education, Youth and Sports with the title “first aid member” lasting at least 80 teaching hours,

- g) instructors of the Czech Red Cross and graduates of medical preparation focused on teaching in driving schools.

p23.text:
(1) Examinations for teaching and training instructors are conducted by the regional authority. Basic training may be provided by a driving school within the scope of its registration for operating instruction and training of applicants for a driving license. Basic training is provided at the applicant's expense.

(2) Before commencing independent teaching activity, a future teaching and training instructor must undergo practical training under the guidance of a person who has been an instructor for at least five years with the same or broader scope of professional certification. The scope of practical training must be at least the extent of the weekly working hours stipulated by a special legal regulation.^2c)

(3) The curriculum for basic training, the method of conducting and organizing the examination for teaching and training instructors shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p24.text:
(1) The Regional Authority shall decide on the revocation of a professional certificate if its holder

- a) repeatedly or grossly violated the curricula, or

- b) lost the conditions under which the professional certificate was issued.

(2) An applicant may submit a request for the return of a professional certificate no earlier than one year after the decision on its revocation becomes final. Before the decision on the return of the professional certificate is issued, the applicant must demonstrate knowledge by examination according to § 21 para. 2 letter d). If the applicant requests the return of the professional certificate after two years from its revocation, they must complete basic training according to § 21 para. 2 letter d).

(3) If the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers revokes the professional certificate holder's authorization to drive vehicles due to loss of health fitness, the professional certificate holder may ask the regional authority to retain the professional certificate, excluding vehicle driving training.

p25.text: Before commencing instruction or training, the driving school operator is obliged to send to the municipal authority of the municipality with extended powers, competent according to the location of the driving school operator's premises, a list of applicants for driving licenses enrolled in instruction and training. The list shall include the applicant's first name and surname, their date of birth, address, driving license number, if they possess one, the type of instruction and training, and the vehicle groups for which the instruction and training of applicants will be conducted, the address of the classrooms where instruction will be conducted, a list of training vehicles on which training will be conducted, and the date of commencement of training. Instruction and training commence with the completion of the first lesson.

p26.text:
(1) Training must follow theoretical instruction.

(2) A part of vehicle driving training may be replaced by the driving school operator with training on a driving simulator.

(3) The scope of driving simulator training according to paragraph 2 and the classification of driving simulators into individual groups depending on their technical design and development stage are specified in Annex No. 4.

p27.text:
(1) The driving school operator is obliged, before commencing vehicle driving training, to ensure that the applicant for a driving licence is familiarized with the principles of vehicle control and driving theory, and the principles of safe driving of the training vehicle.

(2) The driving school operator is further obliged to ensure that

- a) a lesson lasts 45 minutes,

- b) vehicle driving training does not exceed 4 lessons per day for one applicant; in the case of training for an applicant seeking a driving licence for group AM, A1, A2, A, B1 or B, vehicle driving training must not exceed 2 lessons per day in the first and second stages of training according to Annex No. 3,

- c) during vehicle driving training, no person other than instructors and a training interpreter, applicants for a driving licence undergoing training, a person participating in basic training for obtaining a professional certificate, a holder of a professional certificate during preparation for independent performance as a teaching and training instructor, or a person performing training control, is present in the vehicle.

(3) Upon commencement of instruction and training, the driving school operator is obliged to issue the applicant for a driving licence an applicant's card and continuously record their participation in practical training therein. The applicant for a driving licence must always carry the applicant's card during practical training. The applicant's card may also be kept in electronic form.

(4) The driving school operator must maintain verifiable records of the course of instruction and training of the applicant for a driving licence and their attendance at instruction and training hours. These records may also be kept in electronic form.

(5) The method of keeping records of instruction and training and making entries in the applicant's card shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p28.text: Before the completion of vehicle driving training, at least one lesson must be dedicated to practicing correct conduct in various risky situations (for example, intensive braking, bypassing an obstacle, or sudden change of driving direction combined with controlling other vehicle control elements). This part of the training must be carried out in such a way that it cannot disrupt the safety and fluidity of road traffic.

p29.text:
(1) During vehicle driving training on a driving range or practice area, the driving school operator must ensure that, in the training vehicle which is mandatorily equipped with seat belts, with the exception of practicing reversing, the applicant for a driving licence is fastened by these belts and that only applicants for a driving licence are in the vehicle.

(2) On a driving range equipped with a radio command device that allows the training instructor from a station outside the vehicle to give acoustic commands to applicants for a driving licence in vehicles and to stop the vehicle, the training instructor may control the training of a maximum of three vehicles at once from this station. If the radio command device only allows the training instructor to give acoustic instructions without the possibility of stopping the vehicle, they may control a maximum of two training vehicles at once from a station outside the vehicle. In such a case, the motorcycle does not need to be equipped with dual steering and dual control of the service brake and clutch. The radio command device may only be used on a driving range.

p29a.text: During training for obtaining a driving licence for group AM, A1, A2 or A conducted in road traffic, a motorcycle that is not equipped with dual steering or a dual device allowing control of the clutch and service brake may be used as a training vehicle. In such a case, the applicant for a driving licence must be accompanied by a training instructor on an accompanying motorcycle or in an accompanying passenger car, and communication between the instructor and the applicant must be ensured by means of an electronic device allowing communication between the instructor and the student; in this way, the training instructor may accompany only 1 applicant for a driving licence, and the accompanying vehicle must not be driven by another applicant for a driving licence.

p30.text:
(1) The driving school operator shall terminate the instruction and training of an applicant for a driving licence provided that the applicant has completed the minimum number of hours of instruction and training stipulated by the curriculum for the relevant vehicle group, or a higher number of hours agreed upon between the applicant for a driving licence and the driving school operator, and possesses the necessary knowledge and skills required for the professional competence examination for driving motor vehicles. If the applicant insists on terminating the instruction and training and on applying for the examination, even though they do not possess the necessary knowledge and skills according to the preceding sentence, the driving school operator shall terminate the instruction and training based on their written request; if the person is under 18 years of age, the instruction and training shall be terminated only with the written consent of their legal guardian.

(2) In the event of premature termination of an applicant's instruction and training with a driving school operator, the applicant may continue instruction and training with another driving school operator. In such a case, the driving school operator is obliged to issue the applicant a certificate to substantiate the data necessary for continuing the training. The details of the certificate shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p31.text:
(1) An applicant for a driving licence who has completed vocational education in a mechanical, transport, or automotive repair field, or in agricultural and forestry mechanization at a secondary school or university, is not required to participate in practical vehicle maintenance training.

(2) Persons listed in § 22 are not required to participate in first aid training.

(3) In the cases specified in paragraphs 1 and 2, the training instructor designated by the driving school operator shall make a record of this fact in the class register and in the applicant's card. The applicant for a driving licence is obliged to attach proof of fulfilling the conditions according to paragraphs 1 and 2 to the application.

p32.text:
(1) An applicant for a driving licence shall, after completing instruction and training at a driving school, undergo an examination of professional competence to drive a motor vehicle.

(2) The driving school operator is obliged, no later than 15 days from the date of completion of instruction and training, or delivery of the applicant's request for registration for a repeated examination pursuant to § 39 para 2 or 3, to register the applicant for a driving licence in writing with the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers for an examination of professional competence to drive a motor vehicle to obtain the driving licence for which the applicant completed instruction and training with the driving school operator, or for a repeated examination. The competent municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers is determined by the location of the driving school operator's premises.

(3) The competent municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall promptly inform the applicant in writing, through the driving school, of the place, date, and time of the examination of professional competence to drive a motor vehicle, or the repeated examination. The municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall schedule the applicant for the examination of professional competence, or the repeated examination, so that it commences no later than 15 days after receiving the driving school operator's request for registration for the examination, and shall ensure that the driving school's request is satisfied on a single date.

p33.heading: Examiner's Licence
p33.text:
(1) Examinations of applicants for driving licences shall be conducted by the competent municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers.

(2) For the purpose of conducting examinations, the competent municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall appoint an examiner, who may only be an employee of the municipality assigned to the municipal authority who is also a holder of a valid examiner's licence issued under this Act; in the case of an examination of knowledge of vehicle operation and maintenance and practical driving with a training vehicle, the examiner must hold a valid examiner's licence covering the vehicle categories for which the examination is conducted.

(3) For the purpose of conducting examinations of persons who are active military personnel, the competent municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall appoint a military examiner.^3)

(4) In the event that, during the exercise of state supervision or superior state supervision over the conduct of examinations, such serious deficiencies are found with an examiner or military examiner that the examinations of professional competence of applicants for driving licences cannot be completed by that examiner, these examinations shall be completed by another holder of an examiner's licence.

(5) The performance of the duties of an examiner is incompatible with business or any other type of gainful activity in the field of driving school operation.

p34.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport (hereinafter referred to as the „Ministry“) decides on the issuance of an examiner's licence. The Ministry shall issue an examiner's licence if the applicant, based on a written application, proves that

- a) they are over 25 years of age,

- b) they have completed secondary education with a maturita examination,

- c) they have held a driving licence for at least 5 years for the vehicle category for which they will examine the applicant for a driving licence,

- d) they have completed basic training for examiners and have demonstrated, through an examination, skills related to conducting and evaluating professional competence examinations, knowledge of regulations on road traffic, on conditions for vehicle operation on roads, on acquiring and improving professional competence to drive motor vehicles, and knowledge of vehicle operation and maintenance, vehicle driving, and safe driving,

- e) they have not been sentenced by a court or administrative authority to a ban on driving motor vehicles and that such a sentence has not been imposed on them in the last five years.

(2) Basic training for applicants for an examiner's licence and examinations are conducted by the Ministry. The Ministry may, based on the results of a selection procedure conducted according to a special legal regulation^3a), entrust a legal entity with the conduct of basic training.

(3) The curriculum for basic training, the method of conducting and organizing the examination shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p35.text:
(1) An examiner's licence is issued for five years.

(2) An examiner's licence must contain the holder's first name, surname, and date of birth, the scope of authorization to conduct examinations of professional competence to drive motor vehicles, the assigned personal identification number of the examiner and the number of the assigned stamp, the series and number of the licence, the date of issue (extension), and its period of validity.

(3) The Ministry maintains records of issued examiner's licences to the extent of the data stated in the licence.

(4) The specimen of the examiner's licence shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p36.text:
(1) An examiner is obliged to undergo annual refresher training for the purpose of deepening knowledge and skills necessary for conducting examinations, lasting at least 2 days, and for the purpose of deepening practical skills necessary for driving vehicles included in the relevant group, lasting at least 1 day. As part of the refresher training in the last year of validity of the examiner's licence, the examiner is obliged to undergo a re-examination.

(2) The Ministry shall extend the validity of an examiner's licence for another 5 years based on a written application from the holder, if the applicant proves that they have completed refresher training for examiners and a re-examination.

(3) Refresher training for examiners and their re-examination are conducted by the Ministry. The Ministry may, based on the results of a selection procedure conducted according to a special legal regulation^3a), entrust a legal entity with the conduct of refresher training for examiners.

(4) If an examiner does not participate in refresher training and re-examination for a period longer than one year from the expiry of the examiner's licence, they must again complete basic training for examiners and pass the examination.

(5) The curriculum for refresher training for examiners, the scope of re-examination, and the method of its organization shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p37.heading: Basic Provisions on Professional Competence Examinations
p37.text:
(1) The Ministry shall revoke the examiner's license if the examiner

- a) repeatedly or grossly violated the duties stipulated by this Act,

- b) lost or had their driving license for the relevant vehicle group revoked, or

- c) performs an activity incompatible with performing the duties of an examiner pursuant to § 33 para. 5.

(2) The examiner is obliged to immediately, upon the legal force of the decision on the revocation of the examiner's license, hand over the examiner's license and the assigned stamp to the Ministry.

(3) An applicant may submit an application for re-issuance of the examiner's license no earlier than three years after the revocation of the examiner's license.

p38.text:
(1) Professional competence examinations for driving license applicants and repeated examinations shall generally be conducted on one day. If the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers does not conduct the examinations on one day, it shall schedule the applicant for further examinations so that they are conducted no later than 7 days from their commencement.

(2) The driving school operator who provided the instruction and training for the applicant shall provide the relevant municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers with a training vehicle for the purpose of the examination, at the applicant's expense.

(3) The applicant for a driving license shall be accompanied to the examination by the holder of the relevant professional certificate; this does not apply to the examination of road traffic regulations and first aid training.

(4) The scope of professional competence examinations for individual vehicle groups and the method of their conduct are specified in Annex No. 5.

p39.text:
(1) An applicant for a driving license must undergo a professional competence examination for driving motor vehicles, which consists of an examination

- a) of road traffic regulations and first aid training,

- b) of knowledge of vehicle control and maintenance, if the applicant is applying for a driving license for group C1, C1+E, C, C+E, D1, D1+E, D or D+E,

- c) of practical driving with a training vehicle.

(2) The result of each examination is graded as „passed“ or „failed“. If an applicant for a driving license „failed“ any examination, they may repeat this examination a maximum of twice. If the applicant does not succeed even in the second repeated examination, they must, before the next examination, undergo

- a) new instruction pursuant to § 20 para. 2, if it is an examination pursuant to paragraph 1 letter a),

- b) new instruction pursuant to § 20 para. 2 letter b), if it is an examination pursuant to paragraph 1 letter b),

- c) new training pursuant to § 20 para. 3 letter a), if it is an examination pursuant to paragraph 1 letter c).

(3) After completing the instruction or training pursuant to paragraph 2, the applicant for a driving license may repeat the examination in which they „failed“ a maximum of three times. If they do not succeed even in these repeated examinations, they are obliged, before the next examination, to complete new instruction and training in full scope. The next examination shall be conducted pursuant to paragraph 1.

(4) Professional competence examinations for driving motor vehicles begin with a test of road traffic regulations and first aid training. If the applicant for a driving license is graded as „failed“ in this examination, they may not continue with further examinations until they are graded as „passed“ in a repeated examination.

(5) The examiner shall record the examination results in the application for admission to instruction and training and confirm the entry with their signature and stamp. The examiner shall communicate the examination results to the Ministry of Transport in a manner allowing remote access. After completing the examinations, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall stamp the application for admission to instruction and training with the stamp of the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers.

(6) If the applicant for a driving license does not succeed in the examinations within 12 months from the date of commencement of the first examination following the completion of instruction and training in full scope, they are obliged, before the next examination, to complete new instruction and training in full scope.

(7) If the applicant does not sufficiently or at all master the language in which the examination is conducted, they may take the examination in the presence of an interpreter appointed pursuant to a special act.^3b) The applicant shall arrange for the interpreter at their own expense. The examination of road traffic regulations and first aid training shall in such a case be conducted orally.

(8) The applicant may use the option pursuant to paragraph 7 only if they had an interpreter available during all hours of instruction for the driving license. The interpreter's participation in the instruction must be documented together with the applicant's participation in the instruction.

p39a.text: The applicant for a driving license is obliged to pay the relevant municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers, where the examiner is employed, 700 CZK for the professional competence examination; in the case of repeated examinations, the applicant shall pay 100 CZK for a repeated examination of road traffic regulations, 200 CZK for a repeated examination of knowledge of vehicle control and maintenance, and 400 CZK for a repeated practical driving examination.

p40.heading: Examination of road traffic regulations and first aid training
p40.text:
(1) The examination of road traffic regulations and first aid training shall be conducted by a written test or using computer technology. The test shall contain questions on road traffic rules, regulations on vehicle operating conditions on roads, first aid training, and also on regulations related to road traffic that are part of the instruction according to the curricula. The examination conducted using computer technology is compiled by random selection of individual examination questions. The wording of all examination questions on road traffic regulations and first aid training is published by the Ministry in the Transport Bulletin.

(2) The time allotted for the examination is 30 minutes. For an applicant for a driving license who proves by medical examination that they suffer from dyslexia or dysgraphia, the examiner shall extend the prescribed time to double.

(3) The number of questions in the test, their point evaluation, the composition of the test according to point evaluation, and the minimum number of points required for successful completion of the examination for individual vehicle groups shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p41.heading: Examination of knowledge of vehicle control and maintenance
p41.text:
(1) The examination of knowledge of vehicle operation and maintenance shall be conducted orally using models or a training vehicle (not images).

(2) If an applicant for a driving licence is a person with hearing impairment, the examination shall be conducted in the presence of an interpreter in the communication system chosen by the applicant for deaf and deafblind persons. The interpreter shall be arranged by the applicant for a driving licence in accordance with another legal regulation^4) at their own expense.

(3) The implementing regulation shall stipulate the scope of required knowledge for the examination of vehicle operation and maintenance.

Practical driving examination

p43.text:
(1) The practical driving examination must last at least 10 minutes in the first part and at least 20 minutes in the second part, if the driving licence is to be granted for group AM, A1, A2, A, B1, B, B+E or T, and at least 35 minutes for other vehicle groups.

(2) In every traffic situation during the examination, the applicant for a driving licence must demonstrate safe control of the vehicle. Driving errors or dangerous conduct immediately reducing the safety of the training vehicle, its occupants, or other road users shall be graded as „failed“, regardless of whether the examination commissioner or training instructor intervenes (verbally or directly) with the vehicle's controls.

(3) If, during the examination, there is a violation of road traffic rules that endangered or could seriously endanger road safety, or a situation where the instructor had to intervene in the driving of the motor vehicle, the examination shall be immediately terminated and the applicant shall be graded as „failed“.

(4) The examination shall be attended by the training instructor of the driving school that registered the applicant for the examination. The training instructor shall sit in the vehicle in the seat designated for the training instructor. The examination may also be attended by a person authorised to perform state supervision, supreme state supervision, and an interpreter.

(5) For an examination to obtain a driving licence conducted with a training vehicle that is only two-seater, the training instructor shall not participate in the direct performance of the examination. In this case, the examination commissioner shall assume the duties of the driving school instructor.

(6) For an examination to obtain a driving licence for group AM, A1, A2, or A, the applicant shall ride alone on a training motorcycle, which does not have to be equipped with dual controls or a dual device allowing control of the clutch and service brake, and the examination commissioner shall follow the applicant on an accompanying motorcycle or in an accompanying car driven by the training instructor. The examination commissioner shall give instructions to the applicant using an electronic device enabling communication with the student; when driving in an accompanying car, instructions may be given through the training instructor.

(7) If the examination is conducted in a vehicle that is structurally adapted for a physically disabled person, or in a vehicle equipped with an automatic transmission, the examination commissioner shall record this fact in the application for admission to instruction and training and shall communicate it to the Ministry of Transport in a manner allowing remote access. This record shall then serve as a basis for restricting the driving licence according to a special law^2a).

p44.heading: Scope of examinations of professional competence for driving motor vehicles in combined instruction and training
p44.text:
(1) The examination in road traffic regulations and medical preparation shall be conducted as a single test.

(2) For the examination in vehicle control and maintenance, the questions set for individual vehicle groups shall be added together.

(3) The practical driving examination shall be conducted on vehicles from each vehicle group for which combined instruction and training was provided.

p45.heading: Re-examination of professional competence to drive motor vehicles
p45.text: The provisions on the examination of professional competence for applicants for driving licences shall apply mutatis mutandis to the re-examination of professional competence to drive motor vehicles pursuant to a special act^2a). The costs of the examination shall be borne by the applicant.

p45a.heading: Re-examination of professional competence to drive motor vehicles in the points system
p45a.text:
(1) A driver who, by reaching 12 points in the points system pursuant to a special legal regulation^2a), has lost their driving licence and intends to apply for the return of the driving licence pursuant to a special legal regulation^2a), is obliged to undergo a re-examination of professional competence to drive motor vehicles.

(2) For the re-examination of professional competence to drive motor vehicles of a driver referred to in paragraph 1, § 45 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

p45b.heading: Supplementary examination
p45b.text:
(1) An applicant for a driving licence is obliged to undergo only a supplementary examination, which shall be conducted to the same extent and in the same manner as the practical driving examination pursuant to § 42, if the applicant is an applicant for a driving licence

- a) for group A2, who has held a driving licence for group A1 for at least 2 years, or

- b) for group A, who has held a driving licence for group A2 for at least 2 years.

(2) A supplementary examination for the cancellation of a driving licence restriction limited pursuant to a special act^2a) (for vehicles with automatic transmission) shall be conducted to the same extent and in the same manner as the practical driving examination pursuant to § 42 of this Act.

(3) For the extension of the scope of a driving licence for group B to vehicle combinations consisting of a group B vehicle and a trailer with a maximum authorised mass exceeding 750 kg, whose maximum authorised mass exceeds 3,500 kg but does not exceed 4,250 kg, an applicant for a driving licence who has not undergone a motor vehicle driving examination with such a vehicle combination is obliged to undergo a supplementary examination, which shall be conducted to the same extent and in the same manner as the practical driving examination pursuant to § 42.

p46.text:
(1) The improvement of the professional competence of drivers of vehicles classified in groups C1, C1+E, C, C+E, D1, D1+E, D or D+E includes initial training and subsequent regular training.

(2) Regular training is a deepening of qualification pursuant to a special legal regulation^6a).

p47.heading: Initial training
p47.text:
(1) Initial training shall be conducted in the form of instruction and training and shall conclude with an examination of drivers' professional competence. The subject of instruction and training is the acquisition and deepening of knowledge in

- a) advanced rational driving theory and principles of safe and defensive driving,

- b) application of national and international legal regulations relating to road transport,

- c) operational safety and ecological vehicle operation,

- d) provision of services and logistics,

- e) economic environment and organisation of the transport market,

- f) social and legal environment in road transport,

- g) health risks and their prevention in road traffic,

- h) prevention and resolution of extraordinary events in road traffic.

Instruction includes a common part for all vehicle groups listed in § 46 para. 1, a special part for groups C1, C1+E, C and C+E, and a special part for groups D1, D1+E, D and D+E.

(2) Training for the relevant vehicle group shall be conducted by driving a training vehicle according to Annex No. 2 under the supervision of an instructor. Only a driver who already holds a driving licence for the relevant vehicle group may undergo the training. Part of the vehicle driving training may be replaced by training on a driving simulator.

(3) Instruction and training within the initial training shall be conducted for a basic duration of 140 hours.

(4) Drivers who hold a driving licence for groups C, C+E, D and D+E may undergo instruction and training within the initial training for an extended duration of 280 hours.

(5) A driver who has completed initial training for groups C1, C1+E, C and C+E and who intends to drive a vehicle for which a driving licence for groups D1, D1+E, D and D+E is required, and vice versa, shall undergo only the special part of instruction and training for the new group.

(6) The curriculum of initial training, the proportion of instruction and training in the total scope of initial training, the content of instruction and training, the scope of the common part and special parts of instruction, the part of vehicle driving training that may be replaced by training on a driving simulator, and the maximum number of participants in an initial training course shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p48.heading: Regular training
p48.text:
(1) The subject of regular training is to deepen the knowledge acquired during initial training. Regular training is carried out in the form of instruction and practical training.

(2) A driver shall attend regular training for a total of 35 hours by the end of the fifth year from the date of issue of the certificate pursuant to § 52c. Regular training is divided into annual courses of 7 hours. An annual course may be divided into 2 consecutive days. Part of the instruction may take place in a manner allowing remote access, provided that proper verification of the driver's identity is ensured. Practical training may be carried out using a simulator.

(3) A holder of a valid document certifying the professional competence of a driver issued pursuant to a European Union regulation^6b) by another Member State of the European Union, a contracting party to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, or the Swiss Confederation, who has habitual residence in the territory of the Czech Republic pursuant to the Road Traffic Act^2a) or performs dependent work for an employer established in the territory of the Czech Republic or conducts business in the territory of the Czech Republic, shall attend regular training for 35 hours by the expiry date of this document. If less than 5 years elapse by this date, the scope of the annual course pursuant to paragraph 2 shall not apply.

(4) A driver who has ceased to perform their profession, returns to it, and has not completed regular training in the prescribed manner pursuant to § 48, shall, if they intend to drive again a vehicle for which the driver is subject to the obligation of improving professional competence, attend a regular training course of 35 hours; the scope of the annual course pursuant to paragraph 2 shall not apply.

(5) The provisions of paragraph 4 shall apply mutatis mutandis also to drivers who have not yet been professionally competent, but whose driving licence of group or sub-group C1, C1+E, C and C+E was granted before 10 September 2009, and, if it concerns a driving licence of group or sub-group D1, D1+E, D or D+E, before 10 September 2008.

(6) A driver who has attended regular training for groups C1, C1+E, C and C+E and who drives a vehicle for which a driving licence for groups D1, D1+E, D or D+E is required, and vice versa, does not need to complete new regular training for these additional groups.

(7) The curriculum of regular training, the proportion of instruction and practical training in the total scope of regular training, the content of instruction and practical training, the scope of the part of instruction that may take place in a manner allowing remote access, and the conditions for the conduct of this part of instruction, the maximum number of participants in a regular training course, and the rules for the recognition of regular training shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p49.heading: Training Centre Operator
p49.text:
(1) Instruction and practical training shall be provided by a natural or legal person to whom accreditation for operating this activity has been granted by the regional authority competent according to its place of business or registered office (hereinafter referred to as the „training centre operator“).

(2) The regional authority shall grant the applicant accreditation for operating instruction and practical training based on a written application, if the applicant demonstrates that

- a) they hold a trade licence,

- b) they meet other requirements under this Act, and

- c) they have paid the administrative fee.

(3) Accreditation for operating instruction and practical training is non-transferable and does not pass to a legal successor.

p50.heading: Further requirements for granting accreditation
p50.text:
(1) An applicant for accreditation to operate instruction and practical training must

- a) have a written plan for ensuring instruction and practical training, in which they define the organization and scope of instruction and practical training, including the maximum number of participants in individual courses, individual instructional subjects, and the method of conducting instruction and practical training, including appropriate teaching materials and, if part of the instruction is to be conducted in a manner allowing remote access, the method of conducting this part of the instruction and ensuring proper verification of the driver's identity,

- b) have a list of lecturers through whom they will ensure instruction and practical training after commencing the operation of the training centre, stating their names, surnames, and professional qualifications for instruction and practical training, who

- 1. are listed in § 22 letters a), b), c) and e), if it concerns subjects falling under health risks and their prevention, or

- 2. have secondary education with a maturita examination in fields related to road transport and 5 years of experience in the field of road transport, or higher vocational education in fields related to road transport and 4 years of experience in the field of road transport, or university education and 3 years of experience in the field of road transport, if it concerns other subjects,

- 3. meet the conditions pursuant to § 21 paragraph 3, if it concerns the provision of practical training,

(hereinafter referred to as „professionally competent persons“),

- c) have the right to use non-residential premises and equipment necessary for providing instruction,

- d) have the right to use training vehicles necessary for providing practical training, and

- e) have an established control system for monitoring the compliance of the provision of instruction and practical training with the plan pursuant to letter a).

(2) If the applicant for accreditation to operate instruction and practical training is a legal entity performing the activity of a school, the experience in the field of road transport pursuant to paragraph 1 letter b) point 2 may be replaced by experience in teaching in the field of road transport for persons who are employees of this legal entity.

(3) Sections 9 and 10 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the use of a training vehicle during practical training.

(4) Fields related to road transport within secondary education with a maturita examination and higher vocational education shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p51.heading: Obligations of the training centre operator

p52.heading: Withdrawal of accreditation for providing instruction and training
p52.text:
(1) The regional authority shall decide on the withdrawal of accreditation for providing instruction and training if the training centre operator:

- a) repeatedly or grossly violated the obligations stipulated by this Act;

- b) ceased to meet the condition for granting accreditation according to § 49 para. 2 letter a); or

- c) requested the withdrawal of accreditation.

(2) The operator whose accreditation for providing instruction and training was withdrawn according to paragraph 1 letter a) may re-apply to the regional authority for the granting of accreditation for providing instruction and training no earlier than 3 years after the decision on the withdrawal of accreditation for providing instruction and training became final.

p52a.heading: Conditions for admission to instruction and training
p52a.text:
(1) The training centre operator may admit to instruction and training within the scope of initial training only a person who:

- a) submits a written application;

- b) holds a driving licence for the relevant vehicle group or is undergoing instruction and training to obtain it.

(2) For the admission of a person to instruction and training within the scope of regular training, the condition specified in paragraph 1 letter a) applies.

p52b.heading: Examination
p52b.text:
(1) A driver shall submit a written application for taking the examination to the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers having jurisdiction according to the location of the training centre operator's premises where the driver completed instruction and training within the scope of improving professional competence.

(2) An applicant for taking the examination must prove that

- a) they completed instruction and training within the scope of initial training in full and supplementary instruction, if so stipulated by this Act,

- b) they paid the administrative fee,

- c) they are a citizen of

- 1. a Member State of the European Union or a State Party to the Agreement on the European Economic Area (hereinafter referred to as a „Member State“) or the Swiss Confederation and have habitual residence in the territory of the Czech Republic according to the Road Traffic Act^2a), or

- 2. a State other than a Member State or the Swiss Confederation and perform dependent work^5) for an employer established in the territory of the Czech Republic or conduct business in the territory of the Czech Republic, and

- d) they hold a driving licence for the relevant vehicle group.

(3) The municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall, without undue delay, inform the applicant in writing of the place, date, and time of the examination. The examination must take place no later than 30 days after the delivery of the application for taking the examination and the submission of all facts according to paragraph 2 to the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers, but no earlier than the fifth working day from the date of notification of the place, date, and time of the examination according to the first sentence.

(4) The examination shall be conducted in written form and shall verify knowledge in all areas of instruction listed in § 47 (1).

(5) The examination shall be conducted by the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers through an examination commissioner according to § 33 (2). The provisions of § 33 (4) and (5) and § 34 to 37 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the examination commissioner.

(6) If a driver fails the examination, they may repeat the examination no more than three times. If a driver fails the second repeated examination, they must undergo repeated instruction in the subject in which they failed, and take the examination. If a driver fails even the third repeated examination, they are obliged to attend new initial training in full. The driver must pass the repeated examinations no later than 1 year from the date of the first examination, otherwise they must undergo new initial training in full.

(7) The municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers that conducted the examination shall, without undue delay after evaluating the examination results, issue a written confirmation of passing the examination to the applicant and indicate its result therein.

(8) The content, scope, method of conducting the examination, evaluation of its results, and the template for the confirmation of passing the examination shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p52c.heading: Driver's professional competence card
p52c.text:
(1) The municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall decide, upon a driver's written request, that the driver is professionally competent if they hold a driving licence for the relevant vehicle category and

- a) have successfully passed an examination within 6 months before submitting the request, or

- b) have undergone instruction and training within the scope of regular training in full and in the manner prescribed in § 48 and have their usual residence in the Czech Republic according to the Road Traffic Act^2a), perform dependent work for an employer established in the Czech Republic, or conduct business in the Czech Republic.

(2) The applicant who does not have permanent residence in the Czech Republic must prove the fulfilment of the condition of usual residence, performance of work, or business activity according to paragraph 1 letter b).

(3) If the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers grants the request, instead of a written decision, it shall issue

- a) a driver's professional competence card, if the driver does not hold a driving licence issued by the Czech Republic or does not have their usual residence in the Czech Republic according to the Road Traffic Act^2a), or

- b) a driving licence with a record of the driver's professional competence; the applicant shall request its issuance in accordance with the Road Traffic Act^2a) simultaneously with submitting the request according to paragraph 1.

(4) A person who has permanent residence in the Czech Republic and holds a driving licence issued by the Czech Republic may also submit a request according to paragraph 1 in a manner allowing remote access via a user interface specified by the Ministry. In such a case, the applicant must be identified by means of electronic identification according to legal regulations governing electronic identification, and the request may be decided automatically without the participation of an official. If the conditions for granting such a request are not met in full, the applicant shall be immediately informed in a manner allowing remote access via the user interface specified by the Ministry, and the relevant municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall not decide on the request.

(5) The validity of a driver's professional competence card or a record of driver's professional competence in a driving licence is 5 years.

(6) The holder of a driver's professional competence card is obliged to report its loss, theft, damage, or destruction to the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers without undue delay. Without undue delay after the report, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall issue the driver a confirmation of the loss, theft, damage, or destruction of the driver's professional competence card. The confirmation replaces the lost, stolen, damaged, or destroyed driver's professional competence card and is valid for 30 days from its issuance.

(7) For a lost, stolen, damaged, or destroyed driver's professional competence card, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall issue a duplicate card to the driver within 20 days from the date of submission of a written request and after payment of the administrative fee.

(8) If there is a change in the data on the driver's professional competence card, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall issue the driver a new driver's professional competence card upon written request. The applicant must prove the change in data. The expiry date of the new card must coincide with the expiry date of the card being replaced by the new card. This provision shall apply mutatis mutandis to the holder of a valid document certifying driver's professional competence issued by another Member State or the Swiss Confederation, who has their usual residence in the Czech Republic according to the Road Traffic Act^2a) or who has started performing dependent work for an employer established in the Czech Republic or conducting business in the Czech Republic.

(9) For the purpose of keeping records in the driver's register and issuing a driver's professional competence card, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall obtain a digitized photograph of the applicant and a digitized handwritten signature of the applicant when submitting a request according to paragraph 1, 7 or 8. If a digitized photograph of the applicant cannot be obtained from the driver's register, the civil identity card register, the travel document register, or the agenda information system for foreigners, or if there has been a substantial change in the applicant's appearance, the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers shall take it when submitting the request.

(10) Any municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers is competent to decide on the driver's professional competence according to paragraph 1 and to perform acts according to paragraph 7 or 8. The municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers that conducted proceedings according to paragraph 1 or performed an act according to paragraph 7 or 8 shall enter the related data into the driver's register.

(11) The holder of a driver's professional competence card is obliged, upon receipt of a driving licence with a record of driver's professional competence or a new driver's professional competence card, to surrender the existing card or confirmation of loss, theft, damage, or destruction of the driver's professional competence card to the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers, if they were issued to him.

(12) The specimen of the driver's professional competence card and the specimen of the confirmation of loss, theft, damage, or destruction of the driver's professional competence card shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p52d.heading: Basic Provisions
p52d.text:
The "improvement of professional competence of drivers" who have points recorded in the driver's register for conduct classified in the point evaluation of breaches of duties according to a special legal regulation^2a) means the voluntary participation of a driver, who does not have more than 10 points recorded in the driver's register for breaches of legal regulations evaluated according to a special legal regulation^2a) with less than 6 points, in safe driving training.

Safe Driving Centre

p52e.heading: Authorization for the improvement of professional competence of drivers who have points recorded in the driver's register
p52e.text:
(1) The improvement of professional competence of drivers who have points recorded in the driver's register shall be carried out by the operator of a training centre to whom the regional authority has granted higher accreditation for operating safe driving training (hereinafter referred to as "safe driving centre operator").

(2) The regional authority shall grant higher accreditation for operating safe driving training to an applicant on the basis of a written application, if the applicant proves that

- a) they have been granted accreditation for operating instruction and training under this Act,

- b) they meet other requirements under this Act, and

- c) they have paid the administrative fee.

(3) Higher accreditation for operating safe driving training is non-transferable and does not pass to a legal successor.

p52f.heading: Other requirements for granting higher accreditation for operating safe driving training
p52f.text:
(1) An applicant for higher accreditation for operating safe driving training must

- a) have a written plan for providing safe driving training, in which they define the organisation and scope of the training, including the maximum number of training participants, individual instructional subjects, and the method of conducting the training,

- b) have a list of safe driving instructors through whom they will provide instruction and training after the commencement of safe driving training, stating their first names, surnames, and professional qualifications for instruction and training, who

- 1. hold a professional certificate for at least groups A, B, B+E, C, C+E, and D,
